truth.text: Which person in this group have you vented about the most to someone outside this group?
What's something your partner does in public that makes you cringe but you've never said anything about?
Which friend in this group do you think is the most likely to betray someone for personal gain?
What's a habit or trait your ex had that you secretly miss, and your partner doesn't know?
Have you ever pretended not to see a notification from someone because you just didn't want to deal with them?
What's a red flag you noticed about someone in this group when you first met them that turned out to be true?
When did you last feel truly alone even though your partner was right there with you?
What's the pettiest reason you've ever ended or distanced yourself from a friendship?
What's a decision you made for someone else's comfort that quietly cost you something you can't get back?
What's the most embarrassing thing you've googled about yourself to see what other people might find?
Who here have you mentally rehearsed a difficult conversation with but never actually had it?
What's a topic you and your partner avoid so consistently that you've both just silently agreed it doesn't exist?
What's something you're genuinely jealous of about someone in this room that you'd never say to their face?
What childhood version of yourself would be most disappointed by who you became?
What's the biggest lie you've told yourself about why a past relationship ended?
If your parents could see exactly how you turned out, what part would genuinely surprise them most?
What's a first impression you made on someone in this room that you wish you could redo?
What's one thing you genuinely admire about your partner that you've never said directly to their face?
What's a moral line you were certain you'd never cross that you've since crossed, even slightly?
What's the most embarrassing thing a sibling or family member has done that you've pretended not to know about?
What's a failure you've had that, looking back, you're quietly glad happened?
What's something about your own appearance that you're self-conscious about that you've never told anyone?
What's a small personal milestone you hit recently that you celebrated completely alone because it felt too small to share?
What's something your sibling is genuinely better at than you that you've never admitted out loud?
What's a job interview you completely bombed that you've never told the full truth about to anyone?
What's something you've watched a friend go through where you secretly thought you'd have handled it better?
What's the most dramatic thing you believed as a child that you're embarrassed you ever thought was real?
What's something a parent or guardian did that you swore you'd never do, and you've now done it?
What's a social situation you completely froze in that still makes you rehearse what you should have said?
What's the most impulsive purchase you've ever made and what extremely flimsy reason did you tell yourself justified it?
What's a completely irrational superstition or ritual you still follow as an adult?
What's a compliment you received once from a stranger that shaped how you see yourself more than anything a friend ever said?
What's something you pretended to understand for years before quietly googling it alone one night?
What's a moment from childhood that felt totally normal then but now gives you second-hand embarrassment about yourself?
What's the most honest thing you've ever said to yourself in a mirror that you'd never say in a room full of people?
What's something you're genuinely afraid of that you always laugh off when it comes up in conversation?
What's the most confident you've ever felt in your life and why doesn't that version of you show up anymore?
What's a goal you had before this relationship that you've deprioritized so gradually you barely noticed it disappear?
What's a totally legal, totally harmless thing you do alone that you would immediately deny if someone walked in?
What's the worst job you ever had and what's the most unhinged thing you did while working it?
What's a version of an apology you received that you accepted out loud but never actually forgave?
What's the most embarrassing thing you've ever done at a family gathering that the whole family remembers but you pretend never happened?
What's a strongly held belief you had at 16 that you'd be most embarrassed to defend in front of this group right now?
What's a sacrifice someone made for you that you've never fully acknowledged to them, and probably never will?
What's the most confidently wrong thing you once argued with someone about before silently googling it and discovering you were completely wrong?
What's the most embarrassing way you've mispronounced a word for years before someone finally corrected you in public?
What's a trend, aesthetic, or era of culture you secretly miss but would never publicly admit nostalgia for?
What's the most creative excuse you've ever invented to get out of something, and did it actually work?
What's something about your personality you've been told is a strength your whole life that you've recently started to think might actually be a flaw?
What's a social rule everyone else seems to understand instinctively that you've had to consciously learn and still sometimes get wrong?
What's something you genuinely believed about love before this relationship that this relationship has quietly proved wrong?
What's the most selfish reason you've ever stayed in a friendship that had nothing to do with actually liking the person?
What's a completely normal thing that other people do effortlessly that genuinely stresses you out every single time?
What's the most embarrassing thing you still do that is objectively something a ten-year-old would do?
What's a moment you said nothing when someone needed you to speak up, and you've never stopped thinking about it?
What's a room in your childhood home that you still dream about and can't explain why?
What's the most absurd rule your family had growing up that you only realized was weird as an adult?
What's a personal value you lecture others about that you've privately compromised on more than once?
What's a strongly held food opinion you have that you know would genuinely offend at least one person in this room?
What's the most aggressively normal life choice you've made that you secretly resent yourself for not fighting harder against?
What's a personality trait you copied from someone in this room without ever telling them?
What's one thing you know is true about who you are that you've never let a single person in your life fully see?
What's a friend group you were part of before this one that you left without ever giving the real reason why?
What's a compliment you gave someone that was actually a dig and they took it completely straight?
What's the biggest gap between how competent you appear at work and how you actually feel doing the job?
What's a moment you chose comfort over courage and still wonder who you'd be if you'd gone the other way?
What's something you own that reveals a version of yourself you'd rather nobody in this room picture?
What's a completely made-up skill or hobby you claimed to have on a first impression that you then had to secretly maintain?
What's the most money you've spent on yourself without telling your partner, and what story did you tell instead?
What's a genuinely weird thing you do before a big moment — job interview, date, performance — that you've never admitted to anyone?
What's a moment someone believed in you more than you believed in yourself, and you've never gone back to tell them they were right?
What's a career you'd secretly drop everything for tomorrow if money wasn't a factor, and why haven't you said that out loud before?
What's the most unfair advantage you've had in life that you rarely bring up because acknowledging it feels complicated?
What's a compliment you fish for constantly but would be mortified if someone called you out for fishing?
What's a small daily decision your partner makes that you've been silently overriding your own preference for without them knowing?
What's the most impressive-sounding thing on your resumé that you could not actually do again if someone tested you right now?
What's a food, movie, or song that everyone in your life loves that you genuinely cannot stand but fake enthusiasm for?
What's the most obvious thing about your own personality that you were the absolute last person to notice?
What's a totally harmless but deeply specific hill you would die on in an argument that makes absolutely no logical sense?
What's a version of yourself you became to survive a specific period of your life that you never fully let go of?
What's a version of success you chased for years that you finally got, then quietly realized wasn't yours to begin with?
What's a genuinely weird physical or mental habit you have in the first five minutes after waking up that no one has ever witnessed?
What's a skill or subject you were top of your class in as a kid that you have completely, embarrassingly lost?
What's a version of your future self you've stopped picturing, and when exactly did you stop?
What's a genuinely kind thing you've done for someone that you secretly hoped they'd talk about to others?
What's a phase you went through that your current friends have no idea about and would genuinely not believe?
What's the most embarrassing thing you ever did to look cooler in front of someone you'd just met that completely backfired?
What's one thing you secretly judged your partner for wanting before you two got together that you now completely understand?
What's a compliment you've rehearsed giving someone in this room for months and still haven't delivered?
What's the most aggressively average thing about you that you've secretly decided is actually your whole personality?
What's a life decision you made purely to have a better story to tell later, and did it even work?
What's something your partner is genuinely proud of that you find privately, mildly, a little boring to hear about?
What's something you're absolutely convinced you'd be famous for if you'd just started five years earlier?
What's a money habit you have that you know is objectively chaotic but refuse to change?
What's a strongly held opinion about how other people should live that you have zero personal experience to back up?
What's something your partner does for you without being asked that you've never once acknowledged out loud?
What's the earliest memory you have of feeling like you genuinely didn't fit, and where were you?
What's a wildly specific thing you are unironically, unjustifiably better at than almost every person you've ever met?
What's a version of kindness someone showed you as a kid that you didn't recognize as kindness until years later?
What's something you're privately competitive about that has no stakes, no audience, and absolutely no reason to matter this much?

dare.text: Text someone in this room's contact list 'your name came up tonight' and show everyone the reply.
Send a voice note to your last three contacts saying 'I've been thinking about you.' No explanation.
Let the group write a caption for your most recent Instagram photo and post it for one hour.
Show everyone your camera roll from exactly one year ago. No deleting before you hand the phone over.
Read the last voice note you sent out loud in a dramatically different accent. Everyone listens.
Stand up and give a 30-second best man or maid of honor speech about the person to your right.
Write a two-sentence apology to someone you've wronged and read it to the group. You don't have to say who it's for.
Search your name in your own messages and read the first result you find out loud.
Let your partner read the last five things you searched on your phone and react in real time.
Tell the group something you've been putting off confronting. Then say one concrete step you'll actually take.
Walk up to a mirror, look yourself in the eye, and say one thing you actually need to hear right now.
Go through your most-used emojis and explain the story behind each of the top five. No skipping.
Pick someone in the room and roast them in exactly three sentences. They're not allowed to respond for sixty seconds.
Narrate everything the person across from you does for the next two minutes like a nature documentary. Do not break character.
Draw a quick portrait of how you see yourself right now — not how you look, how you feel. Show it and explain one detail.
Assign everyone in the room a job based purely on their personality. Say the job and the reason out loud. No softening it.
Compliment every person in the room in a way that sounds like an insult. They decide if it lands as sweet or savage.
Pitch yourself as a contestant on a reality dating show. Group asks you three casting questions. No hesitation allowed.
Direct your partner in a 30-second silent film scene from your relationship. They act. You shout stage directions.
Debate, with full conviction, why a completely mundane object is the greatest invention in human history. One minute. Go.
Conduct a fake job interview with the person to your left. You're hiring for a role that exposes their biggest flaw. Keep it professional.
Narrate a fake movie trailer for a film about the most embarrassing thing that's ever happened to you. Title it. Cast it.
Stand up and deliver a one-minute TED Talk about the single belief you hold that most people in this room would disagree with.
Charades — but you can only use facial expressions. No body movement. Group has 60 seconds to guess the movie you're given.
Negotiate with your partner for one real-life privilege — a free pass, a favor, anything. Make your case out loud. Group decides if you deserve it.
Improvise a 60-second cooking show segment using only items visible in this room as fake ingredients. Name the dish.
Do your best impression of how each person in this room would handle being caught in a lie. Act each one out. No names — let them guess.
Recreate your first argument as a full dramatic scene — you play your partner, they play you. Group judges who's more accurate.
Host a 90-second fake game show where you quiz the group on made-up rules to a sport you invent on the spot right now.
Give a eulogy for a personality trait you used to have that you've outgrown. Be sincere. Group can only applaud at the end.
Draw a Venn diagram on paper showing what you and the person across from you have in common versus where you completely differ. Present it.
Stand up. For 60 seconds, speak only in compliments — directed at yourself. Group is not allowed to laugh or react until you're done.
Announce to the group your most embarrassing talent. Then perform it. No miming — actual performance.
Guess what everyone in this room had for breakfast today. Say each guess out loud with full confidence.
Re-enact how you think your partner acts when you're not around. Thirty seconds. They score your accuracy.
Confess a small, petty thing you've been silently judging someone in this room for. One sentence. Own it.
Describe the version of yourself you were five years ago as if introducing a stranger to the group. Be honest.
Using only sound effects — no words — act out the last time you cried. Group guesses what happened.
Pick one person in the room and pitch a conspiracy theory about their daily routine. Make it convincing. They cannot deny anything.
Recreate your first impression of your partner — the exact vibe, posture, expression. They guess what you were actually thinking.
Stand up and deliver a formal acceptance speech for an award the group invents for you right now based on your personality.
Invent a word that doesn't exist, define it live, use it in three sentences. Group votes whether it should be real.
Mime the emotion you feel most often but never talk about. Hold the expression for thirty seconds. No explaining after.
Improvise a 60-second courtroom closing argument defending the most questionable decision you made this year.
Without using any names, describe everyone in this room as a movie genre. Say the genre and one sentence explaining why.
Teach your partner something — anything — in exactly 60 seconds as if they know absolutely nothing. Group scores your teaching.
Pick any two people in the room and argue, with evidence, that they are secretly the same person. One minute. No backing down.
Name the person in this room you've underestimated. Tell them one specific thing you now see that you missed before.
Freestyle rap for 30 seconds about the person to your right using only facts you actually know about them. No compliments allowed.
Do a live weather forecast for the emotional climate of this room right now. Point at people. Use technical terms.
Propose a fake constitutional amendment that would fix your biggest relationship pet peeve. Read it formally. Your partner may not object.
Stand up. Without naming anyone, act out the moment in your life you most wish you'd had a witness for. No explanation after.
You have 60 seconds to convince the group that a completely random number — chosen right now by the group — is secretly meaningful. Go.
Translate the last awkward silence in this room into a full 30-second dramatic scene. Act both parts. Give it a title.
Invent a board game on the spot. Name it, explain the rules in 30 seconds, and demonstrate one turn using people in this room.
Deliver a 45-second graduation speech to your past self. Pick the exact age. Group cannot laugh until you finish.
Choreograph and perform a 30-second interpretive dance representing the group dynamic tonight. No music needed. Full commitment required.
Argue — with complete conviction — that your worst habit is actually your most attractive quality. Partner is the judge.
Using only your body, spell out a word chosen secretly by the group. No clues. You have 45 seconds. Group guesses.
Pick the person you know least well here. Describe their life story as you imagine it — confidently, with detail. They score your accuracy.
Describe the version of yourself you're most afraid other people already see. One minute. No softening it.
Invent a signature cocktail named after your own personality. Describe every ingredient and why it belongs. Group rates the concept.
Lip sync a song chosen by the group with zero warning. Full performance — movement, expression, commitment. Group scores you out of ten.
Everyone writes down one word. You have 90 seconds to weave all of them into a single coherent bedtime story. Tell it out loud.
You are a news anchor. Deliver a 60-second breaking news segment about something trivial that actually happened in this room tonight.
Stand up and reenact — in real time — how you physically behave when you have a crush on someone. No narrating. Just show it.
Pick two people in the room and officiate a fake 45-second wedding between their worst personality traits. Name the traits. Marry them properly.
Write your own two-sentence obituary right now. Read it aloud. Group is not allowed to comment for fifteen seconds after.
Describe your relationship as a genre of film you'd never watch voluntarily. Name the film, the rating, and the one-line synopsis. Your partner reacts.
Choose a person in the room. Draw their portrait using only your non-dominant hand. Sixty seconds. Show everyone without apologizing for it.
Stand in front of the group and pitch yourself as a brand. Name, tagline, target market, one weakness. Full deck. Thirty seconds. Go.
Everyone secretly votes on an animal that represents your personality. You must guess what animal you got before they reveal it.
Recite the last compliment someone gave you that you didn't believe. Say it again — this time like you actually do.
Improvise a 60-second infomercial for the most annoying habit of the person to your left. Sell it as a lifestyle product. Name it.
The group gives you a celebrity, an accent, and a topic. You have 45 seconds to deliver a monologue combining all three. No warm-up.
Make sustained eye contact with one person and, without smiling, slowly explain why you find them intimidating. They cannot look away.
Walk around the room and silently assign everyone a superhero power based on their biggest flaw. Announce all of them at once at the end.
Tell the group a story about yourself using three true sentences and one lie. Everyone guesses which is false. You reveal nothing until all votes are in.
Using only your voice — no words, just sounds — perform the opening scene of a horror movie. Group votes thumbs up or down after thirty seconds.
You have ninety seconds to write a personal ad for yourself — flaws listed first, strengths last. Read it exactly as written. No edits allowed.
Group secretly picks a famous villain. You must defend that villain's choices for sixty seconds with complete moral conviction. Group cross-examines you.
Pick any object in this room. You have 45 seconds to teach the group a fake martial art based on it. Name the style. Demonstrate three moves.
Stand up. Point to someone. Say one thing you've never said to them out loud — but only if you mean it completely. No softening allowed.
Without speaking, use only props in this room to build a physical monument to your relationship's biggest inside joke. Sixty seconds. Partner guesses what it is.
Pick one person. Guess their most-used phrase, their resting face, and their walk — then demonstrate all three back-to-back. They score each one.
Stand up. Using only furniture and people as props, build a physical timeline of the biggest decision you've ever made. Walk the group through it.
Cold-pitch a start-up to the group in sixty seconds. The product must solve a problem that only exists in this specific friend group. Take questions.
Without coaching your partner, mime a scene from your first date. They narrate what they think is happening in real time.
Face the room and recite every compliment you've ever received that you genuinely believed. Keep going until you run out.
Guess the most embarrassing thing each person here has Googled in the last week. One guess per person. Say them all at once.
Write one sentence on paper that you've never said to anyone. Fold it. Group decides — you read it aloud or burn it unread.
Dub a silent 30-second scene that the group acts out in real time. You provide all voices and sound effects. No script. Go.
Sell an imaginary product to the group using only mime. No words, no sounds. Group has 45 seconds to guess what you're selling.
Stand up. Shake hands with every person in the room and say one word — only one — that you think defines them. No repeats.
Announce a fake recall of a household product. Deliver the safety warning with full government-official energy. Group asks follow-up questions.
Walk to the door. Pretend you're leaving this friendship group for good. Give a ten-second goodbye. Then sit back down and say nothing.
Invent a handshake-based secret society on the spot. Recruit one member, teach the ritual, name the society. Group decides if they'd join.
Translate your relationship into a two-sentence IKEA instruction manual. Read it aloud. Your partner rates assembly difficulty out of five.
Stand up and present a thirty-second argument for why you specifically should survive a zombie apocalypse. Group votes immediately after. No rebuttals.
Auction off an invisible object to the room. Name it, set a starting bid, and hype it for 45 seconds. Highest bidder must act thrilled.

wouldYouRather.optionA: Have your texts read out loud to the group
wouldYouRather.optionB: Have your camera roll scrolled through for a full minute

wouldYouRather.optionA: Always be 20 minutes late
wouldYouRather.optionB: Always be an hour early

wouldYouRather.optionA: Find out what your friends really think of you
wouldYouRather.optionB: Never know but always wonder

wouldYouRather.optionA: Be the funniest person in the group
wouldYouRather.optionB: Be the most trusted person in the group

wouldYouRather.optionA: Lose your phone for a week
wouldYouRather.optionB: Lose your wallet for a week

wouldYouRather.optionA: Get exposed for a lie in front of everyone here
wouldYouRather.optionB: Have to admit your biggest insecurity right now

wouldYouRather.optionA: Date your partner's best friend's type
wouldYouRather.optionB: Date someone your friends all hate

wouldYouRather.optionA: Know exactly when your relationship will end
wouldYouRather.optionB: Know exactly how it ends

wouldYouRather.optionA: Have your partner read your old messages to an ex
wouldYouRather.optionB: Read your partner's

wouldYouRather.optionA: Plan every date for the next year
wouldYouRather.optionB: Let your partner plan all of them

wouldYouRather.optionA: Forget every fight you've ever had
wouldYouRather.optionB: Remember every single one in full detail

wouldYouRather.optionA: Give a toast with zero prep
wouldYouRather.optionB: Be the one everyone toasts about

wouldYouRather.optionA: Be the designated photographer all night
wouldYouRather.optionB: Be in every single photo whether you like it or not

wouldYouRather.optionA: Have the whole group know your search history from tonight
wouldYouRather.optionB: Have one person know your search history from this year

wouldYouRather.optionA: Be banned from one bar forever
wouldYouRather.optionB: Be banned from one friend's house forever

wouldYouRather.optionA: Get free drinks all night but have to wear a sash
wouldYouRather.optionB: Pay for everything but skip the sash

wouldYouRather.optionA: Have everyone here read your diary from age 15
wouldYouRather.optionB: Have everyone here read your texts from last week

wouldYouRather.optionA: Know what people say about you when you leave the room
wouldYouRather.optionB: Know what they say about you online

wouldYouRather.optionA: Give up your phone for a month
wouldYouRather.optionB: Give up your closest friend group for a month

wouldYouRather.optionA: Always have to say what you're thinking
wouldYouRather.optionB: Never be able to say what you're thinking

wouldYouRather.optionA: Accidentally call your boss 'babe'
wouldYouRather.optionB: Accidentally call your partner by your boss's name

wouldYouRather.optionA: Trip in front of your crush
wouldYouRather.optionB: Get caught talking about your crush

wouldYouRather.optionA: Have your mic stay on during a private conversation
wouldYouRather.optionB: Send a text to the wrong group chat

wouldYouRather.optionA: Wave back at someone who wasn't waving at you
wouldYouRather.optionB: Not wave back at someone who was

wouldYouRather.optionA: Get caught singing alone in the car
wouldYouRather.optionB: Get caught talking to yourself in the mirror

wouldYouRather.optionA: Have one friend roast you for ten minutes straight
wouldYouRather.optionB: Roast one friend for ten minutes straight in front of everyone

wouldYouRather.optionA: Find out your nickname behind your back
wouldYouRather.optionB: Keep wondering forever

wouldYouRather.optionA: Have this group plan your birthday
wouldYouRather.optionB: Plan all of theirs

wouldYouRather.optionA: Tell your friend group a secret you've never told anyone
wouldYouRather.optionB: Keep carrying it alone

wouldYouRather.optionA: Be the friend who always picks the restaurant
wouldYouRather.optionB: Be the friend who always picks the playlist

wouldYouRather.optionA: Have your partner pick your outfit for a month
wouldYouRather.optionB: Pick theirs for a month

wouldYouRather.optionA: Find out your partner's exact rating of you out of 10
wouldYouRather.optionB: Never ask and never know

wouldYouRather.optionA: Have one big blowout fight and move on
wouldYouRather.optionB: Have small tension that never fully goes away

wouldYouRather.optionA: Share a phone with your partner for a week
wouldYouRather.optionB: Share a bank account for a week

wouldYouRather.optionA: Get caught checking your ex's profile
wouldYouRather.optionB: Get caught by your ex checking yours

wouldYouRather.optionA: Host every party from now on
wouldYouRather.optionB: Never have to host but never pick the plan either

wouldYouRather.optionA: Be everyone's designated driver tonight
wouldYouRather.optionB: Be everyone's photographer tonight

wouldYouRather.optionA: Lose the group chat for a year
wouldYouRather.optionB: Be added to every group chat forever, including the chaotic ones

wouldYouRather.optionA: Have to dance every single song tonight
wouldYouRather.optionB: Not be allowed to dance at all

wouldYouRather.optionA: Find out who in this group has talked behind your back
wouldYouRather.optionB: Never find out and stay blissfully unaware

wouldYouRather.optionA: Relive your most embarrassing year
wouldYouRather.optionB: Forget your best year completely

wouldYouRather.optionA: Admit the real reason you ended your last friendship
wouldYouRather.optionB: Admit the real reason you ended your last relationship

wouldYouRather.optionA: Know how every person here actually feels about you right now
wouldYouRather.optionB: Know how you'll feel about all of them in five years

wouldYouRather.optionA: Have your search history shown on a big screen
wouldYouRather.optionB: Have your location history shown on a big screen

wouldYouRather.optionA: Accidentally like a random old photo while stalking someone's profile
wouldYouRather.optionB: Get caught mid-stalk by the person themselves

wouldYouRather.optionA: Have spinach in your teeth through an entire first date
wouldYouRather.optionB: Call someone by the wrong name on a first date

wouldYouRather.optionA: Send a risky text and get left on read
wouldYouRather.optionB: Get a risky text and not know how to respond

wouldYouRather.optionA: Be the first one to fall asleep at every party
wouldYouRather.optionB: Be the one who has to wake everyone else up

wouldYouRather.optionA: Get matched with your friend's ex on an app
wouldYouRather.optionB: Get matched with your ex's friend

wouldYouRather.optionA: Have one friend keep every secret you tell them
wouldYouRather.optionB: Have the whole group know everything but never judge you for it

wouldYouRather.optionA: Be told the brutally honest truth about your flaws right now
wouldYouRather.optionB: Never be told, even if everyone already knows

neverHaveIEver.text: Never have I ever pretended to know a song I'd never heard before
Never have I ever shown up to the wrong event entirely
Never have I ever lied about reading something to sound smarter
Never have I ever talked about someone right before they walked in
Never have I ever pretended to like a gift I actually hated
Never have I ever ditched plans because something better came up
Never have I ever forgotten a close friend's birthday
Never have I ever spread a rumor I knew wasn't fully true
Never have I ever been the reason a friend group fell apart
Never have I ever read a friend's messages without asking
Never have I ever stalked an ex's new partner's entire profile
Never have I ever lied about where I was to my partner
Never have I ever compared my partner to an ex out loud
Never have I ever gone through my partner's phone without telling them
Never have I ever faked being asleep to avoid a conversation
Never have I ever stayed friends with someone just because my partner liked them
Never have I ever said 'I love you' before I actually meant it
Never have I ever kept a group chat secret from my partner
Never have I ever cried during an argument just to end it faster
Never have I ever broken up with someone over text
Never have I ever danced on a table at a party
Never have I ever lost an item of clothing on a night out
Never have I ever made out with someone I just met that night
Never have I ever woken up somewhere I didn't recognize
Never have I ever been the loudest person at a party and regretted it the next day
Never have I ever crashed a party I wasn't invited to
Never have I ever gotten someone else in trouble to cover for myself
Never have I ever sent a drunk text I deeply regretted
Never have I ever thrown up at someone else's house
Never have I ever started a rumor just to see if it would come back to me
Never have I ever pretended to be okay when I really wasn't
Never have I ever stayed in a friendship I knew was bad for me
Never have I ever felt jealous of someone close to me and hid it
Never have I ever lied to make myself look better in a hard moment
Never have I ever resented someone for something they don't even know about
Never have I ever cut someone off without ever telling them why
Never have I ever cried alone in a bathroom at an event
Never have I ever questioned whether someone actually liked me or just my attention
Never have I ever gone quiet about something that really hurt me
Never have I ever felt like the outsider in my own friend group
Never have I ever called the wrong name during an important moment
Never have I ever sent a text complaining about someone to that same someone
Never have I ever shown up way overdressed or underdressed to an event
Never have I ever liked a photo while deep stalking someone's profile by accident
Never have I ever walked into a glass door in public
Never have I ever pretended to recognize someone I had no memory of
Never have I ever replied 'you too' when someone didn't say anything that warranted it
Never have I ever gotten caught talking about someone over a video call
Never have I ever sent a voice note and immediately regretted every word
Never have I ever been caught checking my phone mid conversation and lied about it
